name: Brookland
description_short: Brookland Federal Credit Union is a member-owned credit union offering savings, checking, and loan products with NCUA insurance coverage up to $250,000.
description_long: Brookland Federal Credit Union operates as a federally insured credit union serving members in South Carolina, with its main location in West Columbia. The institution provides comprehensive financial products including savings accounts, checking accounts, certificates of deposit, and various loan types. Their membership structure is cooperative, meaning customers become member-owners rather than account holders at a traditional bank. The credit union emphasizes accessibility through multiple service channels including online banking, mobile apps, and telephone support via their Prophet Audio Response system. Brookland FCU distinguishes itself through member-focused services including financial literacy programs, balance financial fitness resources, and identity theft protection information. They offer specialized products like Christmas Club accounts, Kids Club savings accounts, and recreational vehicle loans, demonstrating attention to diverse membership needs. The credit union maintains competitive rates on savings products, though current rates shown on their website (effective September 2013) appear outdated, with regular share accounts at 0.10% APY and certificates ranging from 0.18% to 0.70% APY depending on term length. Brookland FCU is best suited for individuals seeking traditional credit union membership benefits, including lower fees than banks, member-owned governance, and personalized service. The main caveat is that membership eligibility appears restricted to specific groups (details available on their "Who Can Join" page), limiting universal access. Additionally, the rate information displayed on their website is significantly outdated, which may not reflect current competitive rates in the market.
services: Share Savings Accounts with $25 minimum opening deposit
Share Draft Checking Accounts with ATM/VISA debit card access
Share Savings Certificates (CDs) with terms from 3 months to 36 months
Christmas Club Accounts for holiday savings
Direct Deposit and Payroll Deduction options
pros: NCUA federal insurance coverage up to $250,000 on all deposit accounts
Comprehensive loan portfolio including auto, personal, RV, and share-secured loans
Multiple account types including specialized options like Kids Club and Christmas Club accounts
Online banking and mobile app access for account management and transactions
Financial literacy resources and Balance Financial Fitness programs available to members
Guaranteed Auto Protection and MBI/Insurance options for loan protection
Member-owned cooperative structure with annual meetings and democratic governance
Telephone banking through Prophet Audio Response system for 24/7 account access
cons: Rate information on website is outdated (last updated September 2013), making current rates unclear
Membership eligibility appears restricted to specific groups, limiting who can join
Limited information about fee schedules despite mention of one in navigation
No clear indication of branch network size or geographic service area beyond South Carolina reference
Loan application processing times described only as "in most cases within hours" without specific guarantees
best_for: Individuals eligible for membership seeking federally insured savings and checking accounts
Consumers needing personal, auto, or RV loans with member-owned credit union governance
Families wanting specialized savings products like Kids Club and Christmas Club accounts
Members prioritizing identity theft protection and financial literacy educational resources
diagnosis: Brookland FCU is best for South Carolina-based individuals and families who meet membership eligibility requirements and want a federally insured credit union with traditional banking services, lower fees than commercial banks, and member-owned governance. The primary caveat is that outdated rate information on their website makes it difficult to assess current competitiveness, and prospective members need to verify eligibility before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Brookland — Credit Union in West Columbia, SC | CreditDoc
meta_description: Brookland — NCUA-insured credit union in West Columbia, SC. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
